content.full_text:
April 18, 1990

Pam Haener
Community Alliance for School Reform
5114 E. Clinton, Suite 107
Fresno, CA 93727

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-203

Dear Ms. Haener:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)

Your letter correctly summarizes my advice. The Political Reform Act places limits on contributions made directly to candidates for elective office and on contributions made to committees which make contributions to candidates for elective office. A recall effort is not an election to office and therefore not subject to the contribution limits of the Political Reform Act. Please note that the Commission has advised that a recall has commenced when notice of intention to circulate recall petitions has been published or posted pursuant to Elections Code Section 27007. (See Roberti Advice Letter, No. A-89-358, copy enclosed.)

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
